2604.23284.title: Au-M-ol: A Unified Model for Medical Audio and Language Understanding
2604.23284.paper_summary: In this work, we present Au-M-ol, a novel multimodal architecture that extends Large Language Models (LLMs) with audio processing. It is designed to improve performance on clinically relevant tasks such as Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR). Au-M-ol has three main components: (1) an audio encoder that extracts rich acoustic features from medical speech, (2) an adaptation layer that maps audio features into the LLM input space, and (3) a pretrained LLM that performs transcription and clinical language understanding. This design allows the model to interpret spoken medical content directly, improving both accuracy and robustness. In experiments, Au-M-ol reduces Word Error Rate (WER) by 56\% compared to state-of-the-art baselines on medical transcription tasks. The model also performs well in challenging conditions, including noisy environments, domain-specific terminology, and speaker variability. These results suggest that Au-M-ol is a strong candidate for real-world clinical applications, where reliable and context-aware audio understanding is essential.
2604.23284.paper_summary_zh: 在這項工作中，我們提出了Au-M-ol，一種新穎的多模態架構，將音頻處理擴展到大型語言模型（LLMs）。它旨在提高在臨床相關任務上的表現，例如自動語音識別（ASR）。Au-M-ol有三個主要組件：（1）一個音頻編碼器，從醫療語音中提取豐富的聲學特徵，（2）一個適應層，將音頻特徵映射到LLM輸入空間，以及（3）一個預訓練的LLM，執行轉錄和臨床語言理解。這種設計使模型能夠直接解釋口語醫療內容，提高了準確性和穩健性。在實驗中，Au-M-ol在醫療轉錄任務上相比於最先進的基準減少了56\%的字錯誤率（WER）。該模型在挑戰性條件下也表現良好，包括噪音環境、特定領域術語和說話者變異性。這些結果表明，Au-M-ol是現實世界臨床應用的有力候選者，可靠且具上下文意識的音頻理解至關重要。
2604.23284.author: Meizhu Liu et.al.
2604.23284.authors: Meizhu Liu, Nistha Mitra, Paul Li, Amine Abdaoui, Adam Ledyard, Tao Sheng
